extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction and category: I counted 6 criminal cases against the Company and 1 criminal case against the Directors; the criminal cases by the Company (47) were excluded, as were the 2 tax proceedings by the Company. Statutory/regulatory actions against Directors and Promoters are both Nil, so the regulatory count is zero; the single tax proceeding against a Promoter was excluded because it is a personal promoter matter, leaving zero tax proceedings against the Company or subsidiaries. The Company’s quantified matters against it are explicitly Nil, so the aggregate Company/subsidiary litigation amount is ₹0 million, or ₹0.0 crore; no subsidiary-specific litigation is disclosed. The contingent-liabilities table is dated June 30, 2022 and reports ₹0.67 million for income-tax matters plus an unquantified Provident Fund item, but it provides no stated Total row, so the contingent-liabilities total is not extracted. The section contains no company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue concentration percentage; monetary tables use ₹ millions, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (₹0.67 million = ₹0.067 crore, though no contingent total is reported).
